sourceMovie.title: How to Train Your Dragon
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit How to Train Your Dragon for the same reason it broke through in 2025: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a family rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Dean DeBlois keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The fantasy and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Maleficent
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If How to Train Your Dragon clicked for you, Maleficent (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Castle in the Sky
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If How to Train Your Dragon clicked for you, Castle in the Sky (1986) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after How to Train Your Dragon?
faq.answer: Try Maleficent, Castle in the Sky, The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring—each shares DNA with How to Train Your Dragon while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is How to Train Your Dragon?
faq.answer: How to Train Your Dragon (2025) is best described as Fantasy, Family, Action, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is How to Train Your Dragon part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "How to Train Your Dragon (Live-Action) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
